flamestrike_decision.statement: the bottom ring right side seems to have some vertical stretching, pushing it above the top base stone ... and there seeems to be some deformation on the left side of the bottom ring as well ... also the top down image does not match geometrically and visually does not show individual stones as the concept art shows

first_drift_action: A07 reused the continuous annular masonry-course construction method and expanded its top crown extents without replacing the shell with source-visible discrete stones or adding course-separation and per-stone UV gates.
invalid_assumption: Correct top-crown dimensions on a continuous ring shell were assumed sufficient to match the original ring geometry and individual-stone read.

proven_evidence.construction_method: each course is one continuous watertight annular shell with simulated recessed joints, not individual closed stones
proven_evidence.uv_method: component-level source-panel projection across continuous sidewalls

affected_outputs.a07_validation: proof only; technical pass does not establish visual match
affected_outputs.a07_builder_renderer_auditor: reference only for defect analysis; invalid as A08 construction authority
affected_outputs.a07_numeric_top_crown_measurements: reference only; dimensions alone are insufficient

a08_first_action: measurement-only audit of source-visible ring stone count, angular segmentation, radial projection, course height order, and top-view stone silhouettes before any geometry
a08_required_controls: true individual-stone geometry rather than a continuous annular shell with simulated joints
C003 maximum crown height must remain below the C002 support boundary with an explicit positive clearance gate
per-stone UV orientation and aspect-ratio gates that prohibit vertical smearing
left/right deformation checks in fixed orthographic and final perspective views
source-versus-render top-view comparison proving individual stone count and silhouette
fresh A08-only outputs with zero A07 geometry inputs
